rationale: Both candidates independently surfaced the same PR-description/code mismatch around trust_remote_code that underlies the ground-truth codex comment, giving them roughly equal partial recall of the sole documented concern (neither reproduces its exact 'breaks kernels<0.15' framing). X pads out with a weak docstring nitpick and overstates the mismatch as 'major' severity despite no functional impact, while Y adds a sharper, correctly-reasoned finding (lock held across full network I/O serializing FA2/FA3 loads) and calibrates severity more sensibly (non-blocking, APPROVE). Both are concrete and grounded with file/line citations, but Y's extra validated insight and better-calibrated verdict edge it ahead on precision.
